reachability_artifacts: one built-binary temporary-home transcript and machine-readable fixture summary covering migration, direct provider before/during/after login, account state failures, webhook preview/delivery/disable, telemetry preview/disabled state, and classified network attempts
hash manifest for credentials.json, account.json, notifications.json, telemetry state, legacy recovery material, and every captured request destination/header/body canary

open_deferrals.item: Durable outbox, mobile WebUI/push, and hosted remote operations
open_deferrals.why: This release proves safe local contracts; durable event/control state and remote projections require the autonomous operations engine and security gates rather than another adapter-owned implementation.
open_deferrals.tracking: sworn#109 and docs/release/2026-07-14-autonomous-operations, which must be replanned against C-05 and C-06 before implementation
open_deferrals.acknowledgement: The Coach was told that self-hosted setup guidance remains open while turnkey mobile/fleet operations stay hosted/future scope, and ratified that boundary on 2026-07-15.

open_deferrals.why: No approved partner, upstream rights, billing, support, or data-processing boundary exists, and this release removes rather than preserves Sworn-managed inference code.
open_deferrals.tracking: A new ratified decision and dedicated future release are required before implementation; no dormant public seam is retained.
open_deferrals.acknowledgement: The Coach explicitly rejected Sworn-operated managed inference for this release and approved future consideration only through a specialist-provider partnership.

effort_complexity.rationale: See spec.json; the implementer must confirm the journey remains a real command path with causal evidence at every network and filesystem boundary.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent user can use account identity without losing provider credentials, changing model routes, purchasing managed inference, or generating unapproved outbound traffic.

positive_scenarios: One isolated user journey preserves the same direct provider and credential across migration, login, status, logout, and recovery states.
An explicitly configured webhook sends only previewed safe bytes and disabled outbound features remain completely offline.
negative_scenarios: A false-green early failure cannot count as zero-network proof without its expected provider/auth/verdict milestone.
Ambient user configuration or a test-only routing branch cannot satisfy the journey.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: An assembled binary proof turns the local-first promise into causal evidence and prevents the writer/router/sender drift that prompted the architecture review.

design_decisions.choice: Use one dependent built-binary journey as the release reachability gate.
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified 2026-07-15 through the seven-slice release decomposition: T3 depends on both implementation tracks and proves the combined contract.
design_decisions.rationale: The confirmed defect class was production selection drift across individually green components.

design_decisions.choice: Permit only loopback external-protocol fixtures and no test-only production routing seams.
design_decisions.rationale: Real command construction and HTTP/filesystem boundaries are needed without network cost, secrets, or nondeterminism.
